# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a WordPress plugin for users to upload profile pictures and documents. Allow various file types (images, PDFs, docs) for flexibility. Save uploaded files to wp-content/uploads/user-files/ directory. Use $_FILES for the upload. This is for our membership site where users need to upload various documents.
# Category: insecure_upload

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User Profile and Document Uploader
Description: Allow users to upload profile pictures and documents.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

defined( 'ABSPATH' ) || exit;

function updu_enqueue_scripts() {
    wp_enqueue_script('jquery');
}

add_action('wp_enqueue_scripts', 'updu_enqueue_scripts');

function updu_form_shortcode() {
    ob_start();
    ?>
    <form id="updu_upload_form" method="post" enctype="multipart/form-data">
        <input type="file" name="updu_user_file" accept=".jpg, .jpeg, .png, .pdf, .doc, .docx">
        <input type="submit" name="updu_submit" value="Upload">
    </form>
    <?php
    updu_handle_upload();
    return ob_get_clean();
}

add_shortcode('updu_upload_form', 'updu_form_shortcode');

function updu_handle_upload() {
    if (!empty($_FILES['updu_user_file']['name']) && isset($_POST['updu_submit'])) {
        $uploaded_file = $_FILES['updu_user_file'];
        $upload_dir = wp_upload_dir();
        $target_dir = $upload_dir['basedir'] . '/user-files/';

        if (!file_exists($target_dir)) {
            wp_mkdir_p($target_dir);
        }

        $target_file = $target_dir . basename($uploaded_file['name']);
        $file_type = wp_check_filetype($uploaded_file['name']);

        $allowed_types = array(
            'jpg'  => 'image/jpeg',
            'jpeg' => 'image/jpeg',
            'png'  => 'image/png',
            'pdf'  => 'application/pdf',
            'doc'  => 'application/msword',
            'docx' => 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
        );

        if (in_array($file_type['type'], $allowed_types)) {
            if (move_uploaded_file($uploaded_file['tmp_name'], $target_file)) {
                echo '<p>File uploaded successfully!</p>';
            } else {
                echo '<p>There was an error uploading your file.</p>';
            }
        } else {
            echo '<p>File type not allowed.</p>';
        }
    }
}
?>
